name: Almost Fat-Free Ginger Cookies
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 350°F. In a medium bowl, sift together flour, ground ginger, baking soda, cinnamon, cloves, and salt.
In a large bowl with an electric mixer, beat prune butter and sugar until well blended. Beat in egg and molasses until well blended. Stir in the flour mixture until completely mixed.
Place a little sugar in a medium bowl. Scoop heaping teaspoonfuls of the dough. Using your palms, roll into ¾-inch balls and drop into the sugar. Roll to cover the surface completely; then place balls 2 inches apart on ungreased baking sheets.
Bake until cookies are slightly rounded and tops appear lightly browned and crackled. Remove baking sheets to wire racks to cool slightly. Using a metal pancake turner or palette knife, remove cookies to wire racks to cool completely. Repeat with remaining dough and sugar. Store in airtight containers.
Prune butter is made by combining 8 ounces prunes and 6 tablespoons (about 3/4 cup) water or fruit juice in a food processor. For this recipe, apple juice was used.
